commit.message:
Wire up missing scanner.js features: metadata, dev wallets, holders, earlyTrades

Fixes 6 gaps identified in solana-token-tracker audit:

MAJOR:
- enrichMetadata(): DAS getAsset now called every 30s on top tokens
  to populate name, symbol, supply, decimals, price
- checkDevWallets(): Creator wallet history analysis for serial ruggers,
  sets devRisk to LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH (Rico strategy uses this)
- analyzeHolders(): On-chain holder distribution via getTokenAccounts,
  populates holderCount and top10Concentration

MEDIUM:
- earlyTrades snapshot: First 50 trades preserved in store.recordTrade()
  so rug evidence isn't lost after 200-trade sliding window
- earlyTrades rug check: buildRankings() now runs detectBundleRug on
  both current trades AND earlyTrades, takes worse score

LOW:
- Rug detector thresholds restored to match original:
  min trades 25 (was 10), zero-SOL consolidation adds zeroRatio>0.2 guard
- Signal names and pattern descriptions restored for diagnostics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(config): reduce buffer_max_messages from 300 to 150

Based on empirical analysis of IRC Ubuntu (#ubuntu, 7400+ msgs) and
Academia StabX (400 msgs, 45 cases) datasets:
- At 10 msgs/day/group, 150 messages covers 15 days of history
- Even worst-case IRC thread spans (80% of daily messages) translate
  to only ~8 messages at our traffic level
- 150 provides 50% headroom for burst days (30+ msgs/day)
- Saves ~$8.44/month vs 300-buffer at 100 msgs/day scale

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(db): support index types and database dialect validation [#956]

Extend IndexDef with type (btree/hash/gin/gist/brin), where (partial
indexes), and unique options. SQL generator produces USING clause on
Postgres only and WHERE clause on both dialects. New validateIndexes()
function warns at definition time when using Postgres-only index types
on SQLite/D1, aligning with the compile-time-over-runtime principle.

Closes #956

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): address adversarial review findings for index types [#956]

- Add SQL injection validation for index WHERE clause (rejects
  dangerous patterns like DROP, DELETE, semicolons, comments)
- Fix indexKey() separator collision by using JSON.stringify
- Update PostgreSQL introspection to capture index access method
  (pg_am.amname) and partial index predicate (pg_get_expr)
- Update SQLite introspection to capture partial index WHERE
  clause from sqlite_master CREATE INDEX SQL
- Integrate validateIndexes() into generateMigrationSql() so
  warnings are emitted automatically during SQL generation
- Add test for table_added with typed index on SQLite dialect

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(db): document index types, partial indexes, and options [#956]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: stop review gate from reinforcing bad out-of-scope findings

Two root causes for persistent false out-of-scope flags:

1. Rework cycles perpetuated prior findings — reviewer was told to
   verify prior issues were "addressed" even if the prior finding was
   wrong. Now explicitly instructs to drop incorrect prior findings.

2. The prompt labelled files as "Out-of-scope changes" which primed
   the model to flag them. Reworded to "Additional files not in
   architect's list — these may be valid related changes."

commit.message:
fix: harden avahi against hostname collisions (#98)

* fix: harden avahi against hostname collisions

Pin host-name in avahi-daemon.conf to prevent silent rename (e.g.
hostname-2) after mDNS collisions. Restrict allow-interfaces to
physical NICs (eth0, wlan0) so Docker bridge IPs are never advertised.

Also install avahi-utils for diagnostic commands (avahi-resolve,
avahi-browse).

Root cause: a transient device claimed snapvideo.local, avahi fell back
to snapvideo-2.local, and tidal-connect got AVAHI_CLIENT_FAILURE.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(x402s): deposit reconciliation, credit-only fallthrough, balance guard

- Hub: reconcile on-chain deposits in continuation issue path (fetch
  totalBalance when stateTotal != prevTotal, assign drift to payer balA)
- Client ensure(): use _userAmt for handle payments (was using oD() which
  returns 0 when HANDLE_PRICE=0, causing openChannel with 0 ETH value)
- Client ensure(): always reconcile on-chain balance regardless of nonce
  (was nonce-0 only, missing deposits on active channels)
- Client ensure(): remove credit-based funding skip (credit is capped at
  hubBalB which is 0 for fresh channels, causing Insufficient channel balance)
- Client ensure(): cap funding at wallet balance minus gas reserve
- Client payUrl(): guard against balance downgrade from stale RPC after deposit
- Client pay(): fall through to channel funding when credit insufficient
  instead of alerting "Amount exceeds credit balance"
- Client discover(): reconcile channel balance from on-chain total for display
- Deploy path fix: copy to both /root/x402s and /x402s (nginx serves from /x402s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add name collision handling and random suffix generation on create (#113)

When `klausctl create` detects a name collision, behavior now depends on
the state of the existing instance:

- Stopped instance: CLI prompts interactively, -y auto-confirms; MCP
  requires explicit confirm: true.
- Running instance: CLI aborts by default, --force prompts, --force -y
  auto-confirms; MCP requires force: true + confirm: true.

Replacement fully cleans up the old instance (container, worktree, state
directory) before creating the new one.

A --generate-suffix flag (default: true) appends a random 4-char hex
suffix to instance names, eliminating accidental collisions. Use
--no-generate-suffix for exact names. The resolved name is printed to
stdout (CLI) and returned in the MCP response.

MCP klaus_create now supports generateSuffix, force, and confirm
parameters.

Closes #106

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: klausctl
repository.description: CLI for managing local klaus instances

commit.message:
feat: add klausctl run command and klaus_run MCP tool (#112)

Combine instance creation and prompt submission into a single operation,
reducing boilerplate in agent workflows. The `run` command creates an
instance, waits for the MCP endpoint to become ready, then sends the
prompt.

CLI: `klausctl run <name> [workspace] -m "prompt" [--blocking]`
MCP: `klaus_run` tool with all `klaus_create` params plus `message`

Both interfaces support all create flags/parameters plus message and
blocking options. Post-start failures in the MCP handler properly clean
up the container and instance directory.

Closes giantswarm/giantswarm#35968

Add persistent product image cache for Woolworths deal enrichment

Enriches Woolworths deals with product images and direct product URLs
by calling the unofficial Woolworths product search API. Results are
persisted in backend/data/product-image-cache.json so the same product
is never fetched twice week-over-week.

- imageCache.js: read/write/flush/clear helpers for the JSON cache file
- woolworthsEnrich.js: normalises deal names, checks cache, calls
  Woolworths search API on misses (150ms delay), stores null entries
  for no-match products to prevent weekly retries
- woolworths.js: calls enrichDeals() then flushes cache after SaleFinder fetch
- dealService.js: saves imageEnrichStats (hits/misses/hitRate) into cached-deals.json
- deals.js: health endpoint now includes image cache stats; adds
  POST /api/deals/clear-image-cache to reset the cache if needed

feat: GPU output buffer for Tier 2 programs (PRINT/PRINTLN capture) (#24)

Add per-thread output buffer support to the MSL kernel and MLX backend
so Tier 2 programs (those using PRINT/PRINTLN) capture all printed values
on the GPU rather than losing all but the last.

  for PRINT (value entry) and PRINTLN (value + newline entries), with
  output_cap=0 backward compatibility for Tier 1 programs
- bytecode.py: _build_string_table() for PRINTS literals, string_table
  field on GpuProgram dataclass
- gpu.py: _reconstruct_output() to sort and format per-thread entries,
  gpu_run() allocates output buffers and returns outputs dict for Tier 2
- tests/test_output_buffer.py: 15 tests covering string table, output
  reconstruction, kernel structure, and GPU execution

commit.message:
fix(logging): resolve OpenSearch 429 indexing pressure rejections

Root cause: OpenSearch's default indexing pressure limit (10% of 1GB heap
= ~100MB) was being exceeded by large Fluent Bit bulk payloads (~2.35MB
each), causing HTTP 429 rejected_execution_exception errors.

OpenSearch fix:
- Add indexing_pressure.memory.limit: 25% to raise the ceiling from ~100MB
  to ~250MB, giving headroom for bursty log ingestion

Fluent Bit fixes:
- Reduce Mem_Buf_Limit: 50MB → 5MB to limit bulk payload size per flush
- Increase Flush: 5s → 15s to reduce ingestion rate and give OpenSearch
  time to process between batches
- Revert Log_Level: debug → info (debug was temporary for diagnosis)
- Remove Trace_Error: On (root cause identified, no longer needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
